layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode with a score of just 22 out of 100, and most altcoins have been selling off hard over the past week. Bitcoin is holding relatively flat around $62,600 but dominance is high at 56%, meaning money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin. None of the swing trade candidates present a compelling enough setup with clear technical confirmation to justify risking capital today.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines and wait. Keep watching for altcoins that form clear bottoms with rising volume. The fear will eventually create real opportunities, but we're not there yet.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate has significant flaws: DEXE is massively overbought (RSI 85) with a -17% single-day reversal — classic blow-off top risk. GRASS has dropped 32% in a week but RSI is only 47, meaning it hasn't reached true oversold territory and the risk/reward ratio is poor at 0.69x. BONK has a strongly bearish chart (-1.40 bias) with bearish marubozu candles and no identifiable support. MON is in a downtrend below all MAs with only a neutral chart bias. JTO is the best relative setup but RSI at 59 is actually slightly elevated, and the 7-day -16.7% drop without volume confirmation suggests further downside is possible. With BTC dominance at 56.1% and extreme fear persisting, altcoins face continued headwinds. The prudent move is to wait for a clearer reversal signal — ideally RSI dropping below 30 with a bullish candlestick pattern on volume.
risk_factors: US government transferring $297M in seized crypto to Coinbase Prime could trigger significant sell pressure on BTC and cascade to alts
Rising Fed rate hike expectations ahead of inflation data could push risk assets lower
BTC dominance at 56.1% signals ongoing rotation out of altcoins — fighting this trend is dangerous
watch_list: Upcoming US inflation report — a hot print could accelerate the selloff while a cool print could spark relief rally
Whether JTO holds $0.60 support — a bounce here with volume would create a proper entry
Fear & Greed index trend — currently improving from 23.9 weekly average, watch for sustained recovery above 30
market_context: Crypto markets are in Extreme Fear (22/100) with BTC flat around $62.6K and altcoins bleeding heavily. High BTC dominance at 56.1% and rising Fed rate hike bets create a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades.
context_banner: Extreme Fear across crypto markets demands patience — no candidate offers a high-conviction entry with proper risk/reward today.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 with improving trend suggests we're approaching a buying zone, but not yet at capitulation levels where contrarian entries have highest conviction.
US government moved $297M in seized Bitcoin and Ether to Coinbase Prime, creating potential sell-side pressure that could push prices lower before any recovery.
Bitcoin slipping as traders increase July Fed rate hike bets ahead of inflation report — macro headwind for all risk assets including crypto.
No candidate is truly oversold (RSI <30) except BONK at 33 which has terrible chart structure; best candidate JTO has RSI at 59 which is actually slightly elevated.
All candidates show bearish or neutral chart structures except DEXE (overbought blow-off) and JTO (slight bullish but with poor risk/reward ratio of 1.05x).
